content: Combining the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework with multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms reveals an under-explored hybrid design: we can embed inconsistent self-contrast perspectives into each iteration of Self-Refine to generate richer self-feedback without requiring external supervision. This hybrid approach would improve refinement for open-ended tasks like reasoning or creative writing where single-perspective self-feedback often overlooks blind spots, without sacrificing the core advantage of no additional training or external data.
